questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{n})$ denotes the number of different ways in which the positive integer n can be expressed as the sum of 1 's and 2 's. For example, $f(4)=5$, since $4=2+2=2+1+1=1+2+1=1+1+2=1+1+1+1$. Note that order of 1 's and 2 's are important.Question: The value of $f(6)$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f(n)=2 f(n-1)-f(n-2)+f(n-4) \forall n \geq 5 . \\ & \text { Now } f(1)=1, f(2)=2, f(3)=3, f(4)=5, f(5)=2 \times 5-3+1=8, f(6)=2 \times 8-5+2=13\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f(x)=\min \{x-|x|-x-|-x|\},-2 \leq x \leq 2$. (Where $||\mid$ denotes the fractional part function $\leq x)$. Question: The range of $f(x)$ is
questions.solution: From the graph clearly range of $f(x)$ is $[0,1 / 2]$

questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f(x)=x^2-5 x+6, g(x)=f(|x|), h(x)=|g(x)|$.Question: The number of integral values of $x$ satisfying the equation $g(x)+|g(x)|=0$ is equal to:
questions.solution: $\therefore \quad h(x)=k$ will have more than two solutions for $k=0,1,2,3,4,5,6$ $\therefore \quad h(x)=k$ will have exactly 8 distinct solution for $0 < k < \frac{1}{4}$ $\therefore \quad$ No integral value of $k$. $\therefore \quad$ Probability $=0$ Clearly, $\quad|g(x)|=-g(x) \Rightarrow x \in[-3,-2] \cup[2,3]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f(x)=x^2-5 x+6, g(x)=f(|x|), h(x)=|g(x)|$.Question: If $h(x)=k$, where $k \in I$ has more than two solutions, then the probability that $h(x)=k$ will have exactly 8 real and distinct solutions, is equal to:
questions.solution: $\therefore \quad h(x)=k$ will have more than two solutions for $k=0,1,2,3,4,5,6$ $\therefore \quad h(x)=k$ will have exactly 8 distinct solution for $0 < k < \frac{1}{4}$ $\therefore \quad$ No integral value of $k$. $\therefore \quad$ Probability $=0$ Clearly, $\quad|g(x)|=-g(x) \Rightarrow x \in[-3,-2] \cup[2,3]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f(x)=\min \{x-|x|-x-|-x|\},-2 \leq x \leq 2$. (Where $||\mid$ denotes the fractional part function $\leq x)$.Question: The number of solutions of the equation $x^2+(f(x))^2=1$ where $-1 \leq x \leq 1$
questions.solution:
Let $y=f(x)=\min \{x-[x],-x-[-x]\}$
$\therefore x^2+y^2=1$
Required no of solutions $=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f$ be a function defined so that every element of the co-domain has at most two pre-images and there is at least one element in the co-domain which has exactly two pre-images we shall call this function as "two-one" function. A two-one function is definitely a many one function but vice-versa is not true. For example, $y=\left|e^x-1\right|$ is a "two-one" function. $y=x^3-x$ is a many one function but not a "two-one" function. In the light of above definition answers the following questions:Question: In the following functions which one is a "two-one" function
questions.solution: $y=|\ell n| x||$ has graph here an element in co-domain can have 4 pre-images $y=x^2 \sin x$ is zero for infinitely many values of $x$ $y=x^3+3 x+1$ is M.I. and hence one-one function $y=x^4-x+1$ has graph $\therefore \mathrm{y}=\mathrm{x}^4-\mathrm{x}+1$ is two-one
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Let f be a function defined so that every element of the co-domain has at most two pre-images and there is at least one element in the co-domain which has exactly two pre-images we shall call this function as "two-one" function. A two-one function is definitely a many one function but vice-versa is not true. For example, $y=\left|e^x-1\right|$ is a "two-one" function. $y=x^3-x$ is a many one function but not a "two-one" function. In the light of above definition answers the following questions:Question: Let $f(x)=\{x\}$ be the fractional part function. For what domain is the function "two-one"?
questions.solution: From graph it is clear that any horizontal lines cuts the graph at either one or two points between $\left[-\frac{1}{2}, \frac{3}{2}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: Define a function $\phi: N \rightarrow N$ as follows: $\phi(1)=1, \phi\left(p^n\right)=p^{n-1}(p-1)$ if $p$ is prime and $n \in N$ and $\phi(m n)=\phi(m) \phi(n)$, if $m$ and $n$ are relatively prime natural numbers thenQuestion: The number of natural numbers ' $n$ ' such that $\phi(n)$ is odd is
questions.solution:
$\phi\left(p^n\right)$ is odd $\Rightarrow p^{n-1}(p-1)$ is odd
$\therefore \mathrm{p}$ is prime, the only value p can take is $\mathrm{p}=2$
$\therefore \phi\left(2^n\right)$ is odd
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: Consider a function $f: R \rightarrow R$ such that $(f(x)+f(z))(f(y)+f(t))=f(x y-z t)+f(x t+y z)$ where $x, y, z, t \in R$Question: If $f: R \rightarrow R$ is the given function then in the interval $(0, \infty) f(x)$ is
questions.solution:
If $y=x$ in $f(x) f(y)=f(x y)$
$$
\Rightarrow f\left(x^2\right)=f^2(x) \geq 0
$$

Put $x=t, y=z$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& {[f(x)+f(y)]^2=f\left(x^2+y^2\right)} \\
& \Rightarrow f\left(x^2+y^2\right)=f^2(x)+f^2(y)+2 f(x) f(y) \geq f^2(x) \\
& \Rightarrow f\left(x^2+y^2\right) \geq f\left(x^2\right)
\end{aligned}
$$
$\Rightarrow f$ is non decreasing for positive $x$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: Consider a function $f: R \rightarrow R$ such that $(f(x)+f(z))(f(y)+f(t))=f(x y-z t)+f(x t+y z)$ where $x, y, z, t \in R$Question: For the function, $f: R \rightarrow R, f(x)$ can be
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { Put } y=z=t=0 \\
& f(0)[f(x)+f(0)]=f(0) \\
& \text { Put } x=0 \\
& 2 f^2(0)=f(0) \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{f}(0)=0, \frac{1}{2} \\
& \text { If } f(0)=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow f(x)+\frac{1}{2}=1 \\
& \Rightarrow f(x)=\frac{1}{2} \\
& \text { If } f(0)=0, z=t=0 \\
& \Rightarrow f(x) f(y)=f(x y) \\
& \text { Let, } x=y=1 \Rightarrow f^2(1)=f(1) \\
& \Rightarrow f(1)=0 \text { or } f(1)=1 \\
& \text { We have } f(0)=0, f(1)=0, y=1 \\
& f(x)=0 \\
& \text { Also, } f(0)=0, f(1)=1, x=0, y=t=1 \\
& (f(0)+f(z))(f(1)+f(1))=f(-z)+f(z) \\
& \Rightarrow 2 f(z)=f(-z)+f(z) \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{z})=\mathrm{f}(-\mathrm{z})
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider $f: \mathrm{R} \rightarrow \mathrm{R}$ be a function satisfying $f(2-\mathrm{x})=f(2+\mathrm{x})$ and $f(20-\mathrm{x})=f(\mathrm{x}) \forall \mathrm{x} \in \mathrm{R}$.
Statement-1: If $\int_4^{20} f(x) \mathrm{d} x=10$, then $\int_{-9}^{151} f(x) \mathrm{d} x=90$.
and
Statement-2 : If $f(x)$ is periodic with period $T$, then $\int_{\mathrm{a}}^{\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{nT}} f(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx}=\mathrm{n} \int_0^{\mathrm{T}} f(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx}, \mathrm{a} \in \mathrm{R} \& \mathrm{n} \in \mathrm{I}$.
options: Statement-1 is True, Statement-2 is True ; Statement-2 is a correct explanation for Statement-1.
Statement-1 is True, Statement-2 is True ; Statement-2 is NOT a correct explanation for Statement-1.
Statement-1 is True, Statement-2 is False.
Statement-1 is False, Statement-2 is True.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& f(2-x)=f(2+x) \\
& x \rightarrow x+2 \\
& f(-x)=f(x+4) \\
& f(20-x)=f(x) \\
& x \rightarrow x+4 \\
& f(16-x)=f(x+4)
\end{aligned}$

From (i) \& (ii)
$f(-x)=f(16-x)$
$f(x)=f(x+16) \quad \therefore \quad$ Period of $f(x)$ is 16 . Statement-1 : $\int_4^{4+16} f(x) \mathrm{d} x=\int_0^{16} f(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx}=10$
$\int_{-9}^{-9+160} f(x) d x=10 \int_0^{16} f(x) d x=100$
$\therefore \quad$ Statement- 1 is false.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph Consider the functions, $\begin{aligned} f(x) & =\cos ^{-1} x-\sin ^{-1} x \\ g(x) & =\sec ^{-1} x-\operatorname{cosec}^{-1} x-\frac{\pi}{2} \\ \& \quad h(x) & =m x \end{aligned}$ On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions.Question Number of solution(s) of the equation $f(x)=\tan ^{-1} x$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \cos ^{-1} x-\sin ^{-1} x=\tan ^{-1} x \\ & 2 \cos ^{-1} x-\frac{\pi}{2}=\tan ^{-1} x\end{aligned}$
Exactly one solution.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph Consider the functions, $\begin{aligned} f(x) & =\cos ^{-1} x-\sin ^{-1} x \\ g(x) & =\sec ^{-1} x-\operatorname{cosec}^{-1} x-\frac{\pi}{2} \\ \& \quad h(x) & =m x \end{aligned}$ On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions.Question If the equation $g(x)=h(x)$ has exactly 2 solutions then the range of $m-$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \sec ^{-1} x-\operatorname{cosec}^{-1} x-\pi / 2=m x \\ & 2 \sec ^{-1} x-\pi=m x\end{aligned}$
for exactly two solutions the slope (m) of the line must lie in $[\pi, 0)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph Consider the functions, $\begin{aligned} f(x) & =\cos ^{-1} x-\sin ^{-1} x \\ g(x) & =\sec ^{-1} x-\operatorname{cosec}^{-1} x-\frac{\pi}{2} \\ \& \quad h(x) & =m x \end{aligned}$ On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions.Question Which of the following best represents the graph of $\mathrm{y}=\sin (f(\mathrm{x}))$ -
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & y=\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-2 \sin ^{-1} x\right) \\ & y=\cos \left(2 \sin ^{-1} x\right) \\ & =1-2 \sin ^2\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right) \\ & =1-2 x^2 .\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph $K(x)$ is a function such that $K(f(x))=a+b+c+d$, where $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{a}=\left\{\begin{array}{lll} 0 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is even } \\ -1 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is odd } \\ 2 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is niether even nor odd } \end{array}\right. \\ & \mathrm{b}=\left\{\begin{array}{lll} 3 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is periodic } \\ 4 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is aperiodic } \end{array}\right. \\ & \mathrm{c}=\left\{\begin{array}{lll} 5 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is one one } \\ 6 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is many one } \end{array}\right. \\ & \mathrm{d}=\left\{\begin{array}{lll} 7 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is onto } \\ 8 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is into } \end{array}\right. \end{aligned}$ $A=\left\{x^2, e^x, \sin x,|x|\right\}$ all the functions in set $A$ are defined from $R$ to $R$ $\mathrm{B}=\{18,19,16,17\}$ $\mathrm{h}: \mathrm{R} \rightarrow \mathrm{R}, \mathrm{~h}(\mathrm{x})=\left(\frac{\mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}+1}{\mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}-\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}+1}\right)$ $\phi:\left(-\frac{\pi}{2}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \rightarrow \mathrm{R}, \phi(\mathrm{x})=\tan \mathrm{x}$ On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions.Question $K(\phi(x))=$
questions.solution:
$\phi(x)$ is odd $\Rightarrow \mathrm{a}=-1$
$\phi(\mathrm{x})$ is aperiodic $\Rightarrow \mathrm{b}=4$
$\phi(x)$ is one-one $\Rightarrow \mathrm{c}=5$
$\phi(x)$ is onto $\Rightarrow \mathrm{d}=7$
$\mathrm{k}(\phi(\mathrm{x}))=15$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph $K(x)$ is a function such that $K(f(x))=a+b+c+d$, where $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{a}=\left\{\begin{array}{lll} 0 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is even } \\ -1 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is odd } \\ 2 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is niether even nor odd } \end{array}\right. \\ & \mathrm{b}=\left\{\begin{array}{lll} 3 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is periodic } \\ 4 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is aperiodic } \end{array}\right. \\ & \mathrm{c}=\left\{\begin{array}{lll} 5 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is one one } \\ 6 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is many one } \end{array}\right. \\ & \mathrm{d}=\left\{\begin{array}{lll} 7 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is onto } \\ 8 & \text { if } & f(\mathrm{x}) \text { is into } \end{array}\right. \end{aligned}$ $A=\left\{x^2, e^x, \sin x,|x|\right\}$ all the functions in set $A$ are defined from $R$ to $R$ $\mathrm{B}=\{18,19,16,17\}$ $\mathrm{h}: \mathrm{R} \rightarrow \mathrm{R}, \mathrm{~h}(\mathrm{x})=\left(\frac{\mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}+1}{\mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}-\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}+1}\right)$ $\phi:\left(-\frac{\pi}{2}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \rightarrow \mathrm{R}, \phi(\mathrm{x})=\tan \mathrm{x}$ On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions.Question $\mathrm{K}(\mathrm{~h}(\mathrm{x}))=$
questions.solution:
$h(x)$ is even $\Rightarrow a=0$
$\mathrm{h}(\mathrm{x})$ is aperiodic $\Rightarrow \mathrm{b}=4$
$h(x)$ is many-one $\Rightarrow \mathrm{c}=6$
$\mathrm{h}(\mathrm{x})$ is into $\Rightarrow \mathrm{c}=8$
$\mathrm{k}(\mathrm{~h}(\mathrm{x}))=18$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
